payload.source_text: High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.
High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.name: Family Participation
payload.description: Defines the roles, relationships, and reciprocal responsibilities of family members within the household economy, establishing who may participate and the nature of their accountability to one another.

payload.name: Earning Entitlement
payload.description: Establishes the enterprise commitment to value-based compensation, defining what constitutes earnable value, the structures through which it is earned, and the child's right to claim what has been earned.

payload.name: Performance Transparency
payload.description: Governs the enterprise obligation to provide visibility into opportunity, achievement, and outcomes, ensuring participants can see what is available, track their progress, and understand their standing.

payload.name: Parental Accountability
payload.description: Establishes parental responsibility for oversight, financial execution, and governance of the household earning system, including the obligation to monitor, report, and discharge payment commitments.

payload.name: Record Retention Obligation
payload.description: Defines the enterprise commitment to maintain historical records over time for auditability, pattern analysis, and long-term accountability across all earning and payment activities.

payload.name: Task Obligation Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for defining, maintaining, and making available discrete obligations that carry monetary value, governing what work is offered within the household economy and how such obligations are structured and presented to participants.

payload.name: Participation Entitlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for governing how participants claim and fulfill obligations, establishing accountability for completion and linking performed work to the individual who executed it, ensuring proper attribution of effort within the household economy.

payload.name: Earnings Settlement Record
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing and presenting the financial position resulting from completed obligations, aggregating value earned within defined cycles, and providing visibility to both earners and those responsible for disbursement to enable settlement transactions.

payload.name: Performance Record Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for preserving authoritative evidence of completed obligations across time, ensuring historical fulfillment data remains accessible for review, audit, and long-term accountability purposes beyond immediate settlement cycles.

payload.name: Operational Cycle Governance
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing temporal boundaries that govern when obligations refresh, when settlement periods conclude, and how the household economy transitions between operational states to maintain rhythm and predictability.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim earned pocket money for completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to provide the capability for children to participate in the household economy by claiming earnings. Derived from the child actor role and the corresponding goal constraint.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to specify task-based earning structures for children.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to provide the capability for parents to define and govern the earning framework. Derived from the parent actor role and the corresponding goal constraint.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish tasks that carry associated earning value for children.
payload.rationale: Defines the structural relationship between tasks and earning value, foundational to the earning entitlement model.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish pocket money as compensation earned through task completion.
payload.rationale: Defines the structural relationship between pocket money and the mechanism by which it is earned.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide earning opportunities to children.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to make earning opportunities available to children.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim earned pocket money for completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to support children's ability to claim what they have earned through task completion.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to specify task-based earning structures for children.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to support parental governance of earning structures through task definition.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish earning opportunities for children.
payload.rationale: What-column entity obligation: the enterprise must provide the domain entity (earning opportunity) that children interact with.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain task completion progress for children.
payload.rationale: What-column entity obligation: the enterprise is accountable for tracking the state of task completion as children progress.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide weekly earnings summaries to children.
payload.rationale: What-column entity combined with When-column cycle: the enterprise commits to produce and deliver earnings summaries on a weekly basis.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children with visibility into earning opportunities, progress, and weekly earnings.
payload.rationale: Why-column goal expressing a transparency governance constraint: the enterprise commits to ensuring visibility across three distinct information domains as a strategic obligation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain parental oversight reports for accountability.
payload.rationale: What-column entity CCI identifies a structural artifact (parental oversight report) that the enterprise must maintain at scope level.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish parents who specify earning structures and provide oversight.
payload.rationale: Who-column actor CCI defines the structural role and accountability of parents within the enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parental oversight reporting for financial accountability and payment execution.
payload.rationale: Why-column goal CCI expressing enterprise commitment to enable a capability (oversight reporting) for stated purposes (financial accountability, payment execution).

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain longitudinal records of all earning and payment activities for audit purposes.
payload.rationale: Both CCIs express a single enterprise obligation: to maintain historical records over time. CCI-ROW1-C-What-007 identifies the tracking record entity; CCI-ROW1-C-Why-005 provides the motivation (audit and tracking visibility). This is a governance constraint on record retention, derived from Why-column motivation content.

payload.statement: The system shall make tasks claimable by children.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance complementing the actor capability, stating what the system provides at the boundary to enable task claiming.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each completed task linked to the child who completed it.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for maintaining the accountability relationship between completed work and the earning child.

payload.statement: The system shall make task definition with monetary value available to parents.
payload.rationale: System affordance enabling parents to specify earning structures through task definition.

payload.statement: A task shall be associated with a monetary value.
payload.rationale: Structural assertion that tasks carry the attribute necessary to represent earning value.

payload.statement: Pocket money shall be compensation earned through task completion.
payload.rationale: Structural definition establishing pocket money as the earned value resulting from completed task obligations.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain tasks available for claiming by children.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for stewarding the pool of earning opportunities offered to children.

payload.statement: The system shall make completed task records visible to the child who completed them.
payload.rationale: System affordance providing visibility into task completion progress at the boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a weekly earnings aggregate for each child.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for establishing and maintaining the settlement record aggregated within the weekly operational cycle.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce the weekly operational cycle for task availability and earnings aggregation.
payload.rationale: Business rule governing the temporal boundaries that structure when tasks reset and when settlement periods conclude.
payload.fit_criteria: Tasks return to available status at weekly cycle boundary

payload.statement: The system shall make child earnings and completed task records visible to parents.
payload.rationale: System affordance providing oversight visibility at the boundary to support accountability and payment decisions.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain historical task completion records.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for preserving authoritative evidence of completed obligations across time for audit purposes.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce retention and accessibility of historical task completion data.
payload.rationale: Business governance rule requiring longitudinal record preservation for audit and accountability.
payload.fit_criteria: Historical task completion data remains accessible beyond immediate settlement cycles

payload.statement: The system shall make tasks and their associated monetary values available for viewing.
payload.rationale: System-subject black-box affordance expressing WHAT the system provides at the boundary (task visibility capability), not HOW it is realized. Complements the view process while remaining at Row 2 abstraction.

payload.statement: The business shall enable children to earn pocket money through completion of defined tasks.
payload.rationale: Why-column goal expressing a business-level governance constraint on the purpose and design of the task economy. This motivation constrains how the business structures the earning mechanism.

payload.statement: The system shall make completed tasks claimable by entitled children.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance complementing the actor capability. Expresses the black-box capability the system provides at the boundary to enable the claim action, without specifying realisation mechanism.

payload.statement: The system shall make available and completed tasks visible to children.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance complementing the view capability. Expresses the black-box capability to present task information at the boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record linking each completed task to the child who performed it.
payload.rationale: Business-subject structural obligation derived from What-column relationship CCI. Expresses the business responsibility to maintain the attribution artefact behind the boundary, ensuring proper accountability for completed work.

payload.statement: The business shall enable children to earn pocket money through task completion.
payload.rationale: Business-subject constraint derived from Why-column goal CCI. Expresses the governance rule that the business must support the earning mechanism as a fundamental business capability.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a weekly earnings aggregate for each child.
payload.rationale: What-column entity declaring the business artefact that must be kept. Business-subject responsibility for maintaining the aggregate record.

payload.statement: The system shall make aggregated weekly earnings visible to the entitled child.
payload.rationale: How-column process — the black-box affordance the system provides at the boundary to enable the child's view action. System-subject capability statement (what is provided, not how it is realized).

payload.statement: The system shall make child earnings and completed task records visible to the responsible parent.
payload.rationale: How-column process — the black-box affordance the system provides at the boundary to enable the parent's view action. System-subject capability statement.

payload.statement: The business shall operate on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: When-column cycle establishing the temporal governance rule for business operations. Constraint on business behaviour — the cycle boundary within which earnings aggregate and reset.

payload.statement: The business shall make earnings visible at the end of each week.
payload.rationale: When-column event triggering the business responsibility to present the earnings position. Business capability tied to a temporal event within the weekly cycle.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of historical task completion data.
payload.rationale: What-column entity and How-column process capability together establish the business responsibility for preserving authoritative evidence of completed obligations. The What-column names the artefact; the How-column process (viewing earnings and completions) implies the artefact must be maintained to enable that capability.

payload.statement: The business shall ensure historical task completion data remains accessible for review, audit, and long-term accountability purposes.
payload.rationale: Why-column constraint establishing the governance rule for retention and accessibility of historical data beyond immediate settlement cycles. The constraint governs the business responsibility for long-term data stewardship.

payload.statement: The business shall operate on a weekly operational cycle.
payload.rationale: When-column cycle defining the temporal rhythm governing the household economy; establishes the fundamental period boundary for obligations and settlements.

payload.statement: The system shall make each child's weekly earnings visible to authorized viewers at week end.
payload.rationale: When-column event (week end occurrence) combined with What-column artefact visibility; the system provides the capability to view earnings at the temporal boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall reset all tasks to available status at the start of each weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: When-column trigger defining a business responsibility for state transition at cycle boundary; maintains the operational rhythm by returning tasks to the starting state.

payload.statement: A child can claim a task.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing the child's boundary-crossing action to claim work; refines R006 by naming the actor and their real action at the system boundary.

payload.statement: The system shall make tasks claimable by children.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement expressing the black-box affordance that enables the child's claim action; complementary to the actor statement, refines R006 by naming the capability the system provides at the boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record linking each completed task to the child who performed it.
payload.rationale: Business-subject statement expressing the behind-boundary responsibility to preserve the attribution relationship necessary for earned money claims; refines R006 by establishing the business artefact required for claim settlement.

payload.statement: A parent can define a task with associated monetary value.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing the parent's boundary-crossing action to specify earning structures; refines R007 by naming the actor and their real action at the system boundary.

payload.statement: The system shall make task definition with monetary value available to parents.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement expressing the black-box affordance that enables parents to specify earning structures; complementary to the actor statement, refines R007 by naming the capability the system provides.

payload.statement: A task shall consist of a monetary value attribute.
payload.rationale: Structural statement surfaced by object-recursion on 'task with monetary value'; refines R007 by establishing the required composition of the task entity that carries earning structure.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a repository of tasks available for claim.
payload.rationale: Business-subject statement expressing the behind-boundary responsibility to steward and present work opportunities; refines R008 by establishing the business capability to make earning opportunities accessible.

payload.statement: A child can view available tasks.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing the child's boundary-crossing action to view earning opportunities; refines R011 by naming the visibility capability for available work.

payload.statement: A child can view completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing the child's boundary-crossing action to view progress; refines R011 by naming the visibility capability for work history.

payload.statement: A child can view their weekly earnings aggregate.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing the child's boundary-crossing action to view earnings; refines R011 by naming the visibility capability for financial position within the settlement cycle.

payload.statement: The system shall present available tasks to children.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement expressing the black-box affordance that enables opportunity visibility; complementary to the child-view-available actor statement, refines R011 by naming the system capability.

payload.statement: The system shall present completed tasks to children.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement expressing the black-box affordance that enables progress visibility; complementary to the child-view-completed actor statement, refines R011 by naming the system capability.

payload.statement: The system shall present the weekly earnings aggregate to children.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement expressing the black-box affordance that enables earnings visibility; complementary to the child-view-earnings actor statement, refines R011 by naming the system capability.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a weekly earnings aggregate for each child.
payload.rationale: Business-subject statement expressing the behind-boundary responsibility to establish and maintain the financial position artefact within settlement cycles; refines R011 by establishing the business record required for earnings visibility.

payload.statement: A parent is an actor who defines tasks with monetary value.
payload.rationale: Structural statement establishing the parent actor role and its defining capability relationship; refines R013 by recognising the actor who specifies earning structures at the boundary.

payload.statement: A parent can view child earnings and completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing the parent's boundary-crossing action to access oversight data; refines R014 by naming the actor and their real action for financial accountability review.

payload.statement: The system shall make child earnings and completed tasks visible to parents.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement expressing the black-box affordance that enables parental oversight; complementary to the parent-view actor statement, refines R014 by naming the system capability for accountability reporting.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain historical task completion data.
payload.rationale: Business-subject statement expressing the behind-boundary responsibility to preserve performance evidence beyond settlement cycles; refines R014 by establishing the record retention required for long-term oversight and payment execution accountability.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly operational cycle governing task availability and earnings settlement.
payload.rationale: Constraint statement expressing the temporal governance rule that establishes rhythm and predictability; refines R008 by establishing the business rule that governs when earning opportunities refresh and settlement periods conclude.
payload.fit_criteria: Weekly cycle transitions occur at defined intervals with task status reset and earnings aggregate availability.

payload.statement: Historical task completion data shall be accessible for review and audit.
payload.rationale: Constraint statement expressing the governance rule for data accessibility; refines R014 by establishing the business rule that historical performance evidence must remain available for accountability purposes.
payload.fit_criteria: Historical data can be retrieved for any past settlement period.
